steps.title: Find the missing side
steps.content:
The diagonal and the two sides of the rectangle form a right triangle. Let $x$ be the unknown side length, then use the Pythagorean theorem.
$$6^2+x^2=(\sqrt{85})^2$$
$$36+x^2=85$$
$$x^2=49$$
$$x=7$$

steps.title: Calculate the perimeter
steps.content:
The rectangle has side lengths $6$ and $7$, so its perimeter is twice their sum.
$$P=2(6+7)$$
$$P=2(13)$$
$$P=26$$
The answer is B.
